status: CANONICAL. This is the architectural source for all future maintenance, validation, governance, CI and change-impact analysis.

cycle_5_DID: build the canonical architectural model of the repository AS IT EXISTS
derive the module dependency graph (NO PRIOR CYCLE DID THIS)
partition all 127 modules into subsystems (totality proven)
record every architectural claim with provenance
record what cannot be proven, as unknown
cycle_5_did_NOT: run the test suite (forbidden by repo policy)
scope_discipline.remediation_authority: UNCHANGED — Cycle 4's ../IMPLEMENTATION_SEQUENCE.md, ../REMEDIATION_OPTIONS.md and ../CHANGE_INTERFERENCE_MATRIX.md remain authoritative and are NOT superseded.

repository_summary.what_it_is: A closed-loop clip-and-crosspost engine. A source video is ingested, transcribed and analysed; an LLM (a SUBPROCESS, not an API) picks moments and writes hooks; clips are rendered per-account; captions and hashtags are composed per-persona; posts are minted AWAITING OPERATOR APPROVAL; approved posts publish to IG/YouTube (Postiz) and TikTok (Zernio); reach is measured (Meta Graph); and the measurements BIAS THE NEXT ROUND OF SELECTION.
repository_summary.the_defining_architectural_fact: *** THE SYSTEM IS A CLOSED LEARNING LOOP — AND THE MODULE IMPORT GRAPH REALIZES IT WITHOUT A MODULE-LEVEL IMPORT CYCLE. *** The DOMAIN feedback loop (select -> render -> caption -> publish -> measure -> learn -> select) is INTENTIONAL and IS the product (CERTAIN). At MODULE level the domain core is ACYCLIC: 124 of 127 modules are singleton SCCs, and the ONLY compile-time import cycle in the tree (personas x3) is UNRELATED to the loop. At SUBSYSTEM level — an ANALYTIC AGGREGATION — seven subsystems form a cycle, which is REAL COUPLING but NOT an import cycle. *** Whether the mutual-import WIRING that produces it is intentional is UNKNOWN, and the v1 manifest's claim that it 'is not a defect' is RETRACTED (C5-COR-09). ***
repository_summary.persistence: ONE SQLite ledger (WAL, synchronous=FULL), 8 entity maps, schema v11, full-document replace
repository_summary.scheduling: launchd ONLY. No cron, no APScheduler, no in-process timer.
repository_summary.the_engine: pipeline.advance() — a five-phase pass. Every entry point funnels into it.
repository_summary.the_cardinal_rule_honoured: No network call and no heavy subprocess EVER runs inside the ledger lock. Verified at every site.
repository_summary.the_safety_property_that_matters_most: INV-08 — a post is BORN awaiting_approval; publish_due iterates `queued` ONLY; only approve_post (guarded on awaiting_approval) promotes. NOTHING publishes without an explicit operator approval, even on a live backend with the daemon running.

topology.subsystem_definition: An ANALYTIC OVERLAY imposed by Cycle 5 — not declared in the repo, not enforced. See kb/subsystems.json DEFINITION.
topology.compile_time_import_graph_G1: NOT a DAG — it contains 1 non-trivial SCC (personas x3). 124 of 127 modules are singletons.
topology.SCC_condensed_compile_graph_G1c: *** An 11-level DAG — BY CONSTRUCTION. This is the ONLY sense in which FanOps has a layered compile-time structure, and it is an OUTPUT of the import structure, not an ENFORCED constraint. ***
topology.static_potential_dependency_graph_G2: *** compile UNION lazy. A STATIC OVER-APPROXIMATION of runtime dependency — a lazy import materializes only if its function is called. Contains a 45-module SCC; all 19 subsystems in one SCC. These BOUND the blast radius; they do NOT establish it. G2 is a SUPERSET of the runtime call graph, which was NOT derived. ***

the_four_structural_holes.hole: THE SUBMISSION LIFECYCLE HAS NO OWNER
the_four_structural_holes.one_line: publish CREATES posts in a state it will never re-drive; reconcile is that state's SOLE reader and cannot always terminate it. Three independent exclusions each strand a post forever. PROVEN: still `submitting` at +100,000h.

the_four_structural_holes.hole: THE RESTORE RACE, AND THE GREEN TEST THAT LOCKS IT IN
the_four_structural_holes.one_line: restore_snapshot takes a lock in NO domain, then os.replace's the DB. A live writer's commit() SUCCEEDS, its data is discarded, AND its deferred media unlinks PROCEED — deleting real .mp4s whose rows the restore brings back. And a green CI test ASSERTS that outcome and calls it correct.

the_four_structural_holes.hole: IMPORT-TIME ACYCLICITY IS PURCHASED, NOT DESIGNED
the_four_structural_holes.one_line: 107 of 323 lazy imports point to an equal-or-higher LEVEL — 56 STRICTLY UPWARD (true layering inversions) + 51 lateral. `config` — level 0, fan-in 82 — reaches UP to accounts and meta_graph. Module init order is load-bearing and NOTHING enforces it.

the_four_structural_holes.hole: THE OPERATOR IS AN UNMODELLED SECOND WRITER
the_four_structural_holes.one_line: accounts.json / tuning.json / .env are DOCUMENTED as hand-editable. The write boundaries are sound; the LOAD boundaries have no validation. Every load-boundary gap is therefore reachable BY DESIGN.

the_recurring_defect_shape.statement: *** THE DOC NAMES A MECHANISM THAT DOES NOT EXIST, WHILE THE PROPERTY SURVIVES VIA A DIFFERENT ONE. ***
found_in: the docs (INV-01, INV-02, INV-03, INV-05, INV-07)
the code comments (4 for 4 — see below)
*** THE TEST LAYER (AR-03) — the most dangerous instance ***
the_recurring_defect_shape.the_mechanical_tell: A load-bearing assumption written as a STATEMENT OF FACT sits at the exact site of a live bug. FOUR FOR FOUR: reconcile.py:76 ('its status WILL resolve'), providers.py:53 ('no live account routes to an unknown backend'), test_ledger_sqlite_store.py:183 ('restorer blocked on flock'), ledger.py:487 ('a rolled-back txn never deletes a file it did not drop').
the_recurring_defect_shape.actionable: *** grep the tree for comments containing 'never', 'cannot', 'WILL', 'no ... routes to'. Each is a hypothesis the author did not test. And for every invariant claimed to be protected by a test, READ THE TEST'S ASSERTION, NOT ITS NAME. ***

coverage.structural_coverage: 127/127 = 100% — every Ledger.transaction, lock, network call, subprocess, mkdtemp, rmtree, env read and env write in src/fanops is ENUMERATED
coverage.behavioural_coverage: ~100/127 = ~79% — business logic actually read across 5 cycles
coverage.what_the_census_PROVES_about_them: *** They contain NO ledger writer, NO lock, NO network call, NO status client, NO subprocess. A STRUCTURAL GUARANTEE, not an assumption — and strictly stronger than anything Cycles 1-4 could claim. Their business logic remains unread (UNK-C5-3). ***

operational_constraint.cost_to_this_cycle: The gate REFUSED the independent verifier Cycle 5 attempted to spawn to REFUTE its own dependency claims. Cycle 5 fell back to cross-validation (the same AST pass reproduced four of Cycle 3's censuses exactly) — which is WEAKER than an independent agent, and is recorded as such.
operational_constraint.disengage: orchestrate.py stop — AN OPERATOR ACTION, not a code change.
